slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 8 - Research Methodology
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: If a research scenario or project requires: **top-down theory testing**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Empirical Research
Cross-Sectional Study
Deductive Research Approach
Action Research
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of top-down theory testing, you should select **Deductive Research Approach**.

slides.question: Q2: If a research scenario or project requires: **bottom-up theory construction**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Quantitative Research
Case Study Method
Theoretical Research
Inductive Research Approach
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of bottom-up theory construction, you should select **Inductive Research Approach**.

slides.question: Q3: If a research scenario or project requires: **lived experience description**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Experimental Research
Qualitative Research
Analytical Research
Phenomenological Research
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of lived experience description, you should select **Phenomenological Research**.

slides.question: Q4: If a research scenario or project requires: **data-driven theory generation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Descriptive Research
Diagnostic Research
Conceptual Research
Grounded Theory
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of data-driven theory generation, you should select **Grounded Theory**.

slides.question: Q5: If a research scenario or project requires: **immersive cultural study**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Exploratory Research
Ethnographic Research
Evaluative Research
Ex-Post Facto Research
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of immersive cultural study, you should select **Ethnographic Research**.

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Fundamental Research**?
options: It is correctly described as: aims to expand the existing body of scientific knowledge without any immediate practical application.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an observational research method in which data is gathered for the same subjects repeatedly over a period of time.
It is fundamentally characterized by: involves studying and interpreting past events to understand current patterns and anticipate future trends.
It has no relation to: basic / pure research
slides.explanation: Correct! **Fundamental Research** is accurately described as: aims to expand the existing body of scientific knowledge without any immediate practical application.

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Applied Research**?
options: It has no relation to: practical problem-solving
It is correctly described as: is designed to solve practical, real-world problems of modern society.
It is equivalent in purpose to: involves looking at data from a population at one specific point in time to analyze prevalence or differences.
It is fundamentally characterized by: relies on direct or indirect observation or experience as a database rather than theory or belief.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Applied Research** is accurately described as: is designed to solve practical, real-world problems of modern society.

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Action Research**?
options: It has no relation to: immediate local action
It is correctly described as: is a reflective process of progressive problem-solving led by individuals working with others in teams to address immediate issues.
It is fundamentally characterized by: focuses on the development of new theories or abstract models based on existing literature rather than direct experiments.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an in-depth, detailed study of an individual, group, or event to explore underlying principles in a real-life context.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Action Research** is accurately described as: is a reflective process of progressive problem-solving led by individuals working with others in teams to address immediate issues.

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Quantitative Research**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: establishes cause-and-effect relationships by manipulating an independent variable and observing its effect on a dependent variable.
It is fundamentally characterized by: uses facts or information already available and analyzes them to make a critical evaluation of the material.
It is correctly described as: focuses on gathering numerical data and generalizing it across groups of people or to explain a particular phenomenon.
It has no relation to: numerical and statistical analysis
slides.explanation: Correct! **Quantitative Research** is accurately described as: focuses on gathering numerical data and generalizing it across groups of people or to explain a particular phenomenon.

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Qualitative Research**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is related to some abstract idea(s) or theory, generally used by philosophers and thinkers to develop new concepts or to re-interpret existing ones.
It is correctly described as: is exploratory research used to gain an understanding of underlying reasons, opinions, and motivations.
It is equivalent in purpose to: determines the frequency with which something occurs or its association with something else, aiming to find causes of a phenomenon.
It has no relation to: non-numerical thematic analysis
slides.explanation: Correct! **Qualitative Research** is accurately described as: is exploratory research used to gain an understanding of underlying reasons, opinions, and motivations.

slides.question: Q11: If a research scenario or project requires: **correct rejection probability (1-beta)**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Null Hypothesis (H0)
Extraneous Variable
Statistical Power
Pilot Study
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of correct rejection probability (1-beta), you should select **Statistical Power**.

slides.question: Q12: If a research scenario or project requires: **expected relationship direction statement**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Alternative Hypothesis (H1)
Exploratory Design
Directional Hypothesis
Confounding Variable
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of expected relationship direction statement, you should select **Directional Hypothesis**.

slides.question: Q13: If a research scenario or project requires: **direction-free relationship statement**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Descriptive Design
Type I Error
Control Variable
Non-Directional Hypothesis
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of direction-free relationship statement, you should select **Non-Directional Hypothesis**.

slides.question: Q14: If a research scenario or project requires: **confound-free cause-effect trust**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Literature Review
Type II Error
Control Group
Internal Validity
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of confound-free cause-effect trust, you should select **Internal Validity**.

slides.question: Q15: If a research scenario or project requires: **results generalization capability**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Independent Variable
External Validity
Quasi-Experimental Design
Research Problem
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of results generalization capability, you should select **External Validity**.

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Research Design**?
options: It is correctly described as: is the conceptual blueprint or framework within which research is conducted.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a visual or written product that explains, either graphically or in narrative form, the main things to be studied.
It has no relation to: framework for data collection and analysis
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the variable being tested and measured, which changes in response to the independent variable.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Design** is accurately described as: is the conceptual blueprint or framework within which research is conducted.

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Research Hypothesis**?
options: It has no relation to: testable tentative assumption
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a small-scale preliminary study conducted to evaluate feasibility, time, cost, and adverse events prior to a full-scale research project.
It is correctly described as: is a tentative, testable statement about the relationship between two or more variables.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is any variable other than the independent variable that could influence the results of the study.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Hypothesis** is accurately described as: is a tentative, testable statement about the relationship between two or more variables.

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Null Hypothesis (H0)**?
options: It is correctly described as: asserts that there is no significant difference or relationship between the variables being tested.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an extraneous variable that correlates with both the independent and dependent variables, masking the true relationship.
It has no relation to: no-effect assumption
It is equivalent in purpose to: is selected when the primary objective is to gain insights and familiarity with a problem for more precise investigation later.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Null Hypothesis (H0)** is accurately described as: asserts that there is no significant difference or relationship between the variables being tested.

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Alternative Hypothesis (H1)**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a variable that is kept constant or controlled throughout the experiment to prevent it from influencing the outcome.
It has no relation to: expected effect statement
It is correctly described as: states that there is a significant difference or relationship between variables, contrary to the null hypothesis.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is chosen to provide an accurate portrayal of characteristics of a particular individual, situation, or group.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Alternative Hypothesis (H1)** is accurately described as: states that there is a significant difference or relationship between variables, contrary to the null hypothesis.

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Type I Error**?
options: It has no relation to: false positive (rejecting true H0)
It is correctly described as: occurs when the null hypothesis is rejected when it is actually true (false positive), denoted by alpha.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or manipulation, serving as a baseline for comparison.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a comprehensive summary of previous research on a specific topic, identifying gaps in current knowledge.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Type I Error** is accurately described as: occurs when the null hypothesis is rejected when it is actually true (false positive), denoted by alpha.

slides.question: Q21: If a research scenario or project requires: **moderated group discussion**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Stratified Random Sampling
Quota Sampling
Focus Group Discussion
Secondary Data
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of moderated group discussion, you should select **Focus Group Discussion**.

slides.question: Q22: If a research scenario or project requires: **qualitative data theme analysis**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Content Analysis
Systematic Sampling
Purposive Sampling
Questionnaire
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of qualitative data theme analysis, you should select **Content Analysis**.

slides.question: Q23: If a research scenario or project requires: **multi-source validation technique**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Snowball Sampling
Data Triangulation
Cluster Sampling
Structured Interview
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of multi-source validation technique, you should select **Data Triangulation**.

slides.question: Q24: If a research scenario or project requires: **completion ratio of sample**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Likert Scale
Multi-Stage Sampling
Response Rate
Sampling Frame Error
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of completion ratio of sample, you should select **Response Rate**.

slides.question: Q25: If a research scenario or project requires: **questionnaire design validation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Sampling Bias
Non-Probability Sampling
Pilot Testing
Semantic Differential Scale
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of questionnaire design validation, you should select **Pilot Testing**.

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Probability Sampling**?
options: It is correctly described as: gives every element in the population an equal, non-zero chance of being selected.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is data collected firsthand by the researcher specifically for the current research project.
It has no relation to: unbiased generalization potential
It is fundamentally characterized by: selects samples that are easiest to access or recruit, resulting in high speed but potential bias.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Probability Sampling** is accurately described as: gives every element in the population an equal, non-zero chance of being selected.

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Simple Random Sampling**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is pre-existing data collected by others for a different purpose, such as census data or published articles.
It has no relation to: lottery or random number selection
It is correctly described as: is a probability sampling method where every member of the population has an equal chance of selection.
It is fundamentally characterized by: selects a specified number of elements from defined subgroups, but using non-random selection methods.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Simple Random Sampling** is accurately described as: is a probability sampling method where every member of the population has an equal chance of selection.

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Stratified Random Sampling**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: relies on the researcher's judgment to select elements that are most representative or informative for the study.
It is correctly described as: divides the population into homogeneous subgroups (strata) and then samples randomly from each stratum.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a structured research instrument consisting of a series of questions for gathering information from respondents.
It has no relation to: ensuring representation of subgroups
slides.explanation: Correct! **Stratified Random Sampling** is accurately described as: divides the population into homogeneous subgroups (strata) and then samples randomly from each stratum.

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Systematic Sampling**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: relies on existing research participants to recruit future participants from among their acquaintances.
It is correctly described as: selects elements from an ordered population list at a regular interval (every k-th element) after a random starting point.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an interview method where all questions are pre-determined and asked in exactly the same order for all respondents.
It has no relation to: fixed interval sampling
slides.explanation: Correct! **Systematic Sampling** is accurately described as: selects elements from an ordered population list at a regular interval (every k-th element) after a random starting point.

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Cluster Sampling**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when the list from which the sample is drawn does not accurately represent the target population.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is an interval scale used to measure attitudes by asking respondents to specify their level of agreement on a symmetric agree-disagree scale.
It is correctly described as: divides the population into naturally occurring groups (clusters) and randomly selects entire clusters to study.
It has no relation to: geographical or group-level division
slides.explanation: Correct! **Cluster Sampling** is accurately described as: divides the population into naturally occurring groups (clusters) and randomly selects entire clusters to study.

slides.question: Q31: If a research scenario or project requires: **left-tail distribution skew**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Parametric Test
Level of Significance
Mode
Negatively Skewed
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of left-tail distribution skew, you should select **Negatively Skewed**.

slides.question: Q32: If a research scenario or project requires: **peakedness or flatness score**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Confidence Interval
Kurtosis
Standard Deviation
Non-Parametric Test
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of peakedness or flatness score, you should select **Kurtosis**.

slides.question: Q33: If a research scenario or project requires: **distribution-free two-group comparison**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Variance
Degrees of Freedom
Mann-Whitney U Test
Chi-Square Test
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of distribution-free two-group comparison, you should select **Mann-Whitney U Test**.

slides.question: Q34: If a research scenario or project requires: **distribution-free paired comparison**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Standard Error of the Mean
Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test
ANOVA (Analysis of Variance)
Pearson Correlation
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of distribution-free paired comparison, you should select **Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test**.

slides.question: Q35: If a research scenario or project requires: **distribution-free multi-group comparison**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Normal Distribution
Student's t-Test
Kruskal-Wallis Test
Spearman Rank Correlation
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of distribution-free multi-group comparison, you should select **Kruskal-Wallis Test**.

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Arithmetic Mean**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: represents the rate of change in the dependent variable for every unit change in the independent variable.
It has no relation to: arithmetic average calculation
It is correctly described as: is the mathematical average of a dataset, calculated by summing all values and dividing by the count.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a statistical test used to compare means when the sample size is large (n > 30) and population variance is known.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Arithmetic Mean** is accurately described as: is the mathematical average of a dataset, calculated by summing all values and dividing by the count.

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Median**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: makes strict assumptions about the parameters of the population distribution (e.g., normality).
It is correctly described as: is the middle value of an ordered dataset, dividing it into two equal halves, unaffected by outliers.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true, commonly set at 0.05.
It has no relation to: middle positional indicator
slides.explanation: Correct! **Median** is accurately described as: is the middle value of an ordered dataset, dividing it into two equal halves, unaffected by outliers.

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Mode**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: does not make assumptions about the underlying population distribution, suitable for ordinal or skewed data.
It has no relation to: highest frequency value
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a range of values derived from sample statistics that is likely to contain the true population parameter.
It is correctly described as: is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Mode** is accurately described as: is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset.

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Standard Deviation**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a non-parametric test used to determine if there is a significant association between two categorical variables.
It has no relation to: dispersion measurement tool
It is equivalent in purpose to: indicates the number of values in the final calculation of a statistic that are free to vary.
It is correctly described as: measures the dispersion or spread of data points around their arithmetic mean.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Standard Deviation** is accurately described as: measures the dispersion or spread of data points around their arithmetic mean.

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Variance**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: measures the standard deviation of the sample mean's estimate of the population mean.
It has no relation to: squared dispersion measure
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a parametric test used to compare the means of three or more independent groups.
It is correctly described as: is the average of the squared differences from the mean, representing the overall spread of data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Variance** is accurately described as: is the average of the squared differences from the mean, representing the overall spread of data.

slides.question: Q41: If a research scenario or project requires: **inventing non-existent research data**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Fabrication
Impact Factor
APA Style
Bibliography
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of inventing non-existent research data, you should select **Fabrication**.

slides.question: Q42: If a research scenario or project requires: **manipulating active research data**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Abstract
H-Index
Falsification
MLA Style
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of manipulating active research data, you should select **Falsification**.

slides.question: Q43: If a research scenario or project requires: **private interest compromising professional work**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Institutional Review Board (IRB)
Conflict of Interest
Footnote
Chicago Style
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of private interest compromising professional work, you should select **Conflict of Interest**.

slides.question: Q44: If a research scenario or project requires: **thanking contributors and funders section**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Research Proposal
Informed Consent
Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)
Acknowledgment Section
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of thanking contributors and funders section, you should select **Acknowledgment Section**.

slides.question: Q45: If a research scenario or project requires: **standardized academic chapter sequence**, which component or approach should be chosen?
options: Research Monograph
Thesis Layout Structure
Peer Review
Patent
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of standardized academic chapter sequence, you should select **Thesis Layout Structure**.

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Plagiarism**?
options: It has no relation to: academic theft and misattribution
It is equivalent in purpose to: refers to online, free-of-charge access to peer-reviewed scholarly research, removing price and permission barriers.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a legal right that grants the creator of an original work exclusive rights for its use and distribution.
It is correctly described as: is the practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own without proper citation.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Plagiarism** is accurately described as: is the practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own without proper citation.

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Self-Plagiarism**?
options: It is correctly described as: occurs when an author reuses portions of their own previously published work without proper attribution.
It has no relation to: own-work recycling without citation
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a measure of the frequency with which the average article in a journal has been cited in a particular year.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a list of all sources consulted during the research process, regardless of whether they were directly cited in the text.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Self-Plagiarism** is accurately described as: occurs when an author reuses portions of their own previously published work without proper attribution.

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **APA Style**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is an author-level metric that measures both the productivity and citation impact of the publications of a scientist.
It has no relation to: author-date citation style
It is correctly described as: is a citation and formatting style established by the American Psychological Association, commonly used in social sciences.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a self-contained, brief summary of a research paper, thesis, or review, outlining goals, methods, and main findings.
slides.explanation: Correct! **APA Style** is accurately described as: is a citation and formatting style established by the American Psychological Association, commonly used in social sciences.

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **MLA Style**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a document that provides additional context or citations for text on that page.
It has no relation to: author-page citation style
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a committee formally designated to approve, monitor, and review biomedical and behavioral research involving humans.
It is correctly described as: is a formatting style by the Modern Language Association, commonly used in humanities, utilizing parenthetical page citations.
slides.explanation: Correct! **MLA Style** is accurately described as: is a formatting style by the Modern Language Association, commonly used in humanities, utilizing parenthetical page citations.

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Chicago Style**?
options: It has no relation to: notes-bibliography citation formatting
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the process of providing research participants with clear info about the study to help them voluntarily agree to participate.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a structured document outlining what the researcher plans to study, why it is important, and how they will execute it.
It is correctly described as: is a citation style providing bibliography and notes/author-date formats, used widely in history and social sciences.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Chicago Style** is accurately described as: is a citation style providing bibliography and notes/author-date formats, used widely in history and social sciences.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 8. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Research Methodology Mock Exam 8.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Research Methodology questions!
